channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Some US lawmakers want more Christianity in the classroom. Trump could embolden their plans
item.description: Conservative lawmakers across the U.S. are pushing to introduce more Christianity to public school classrooms, testing the separation of church and state.
item.dc:creator: Moriah Balingit, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Florida Gov. DeSantis open to special session for immigration, hurricane relief
item.description: It’s widely known that Gov. Ron DeSantis is open to the idea of a special session to help resolve the condo affordability crisis. However, the governor is also open to the idea of state lawmakers doing more before the yearly scheduled session starts.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump fills latest Cabinet spots as Jan. 20 inauguration nears
item.description: President-elect Trump named Bill Briggs as Deputy Administrator of the U.S. Small Business Administration and Ed Russo as his pick for the Environmental Advisory Task Force.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge holds Rudy Giuliani in contempt of court for continued lies about Georgia election workers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Rudy Giuliani was found in contempt of court Friday for the second time in a week, as a federal judge warned him he could be sent to jail if he doesn't stop spreading lies about two former Georgia election workers who won a $148 million defamation judgement against him. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden claim about empty hydrants undermined by LA water utility's lack of common wildfire safety protocol
item.description: President Biden on Thursday said that empty fire hydrants in the Los Angeles area were the result of an intentional power shutoff, but a report indicates the city's water utility might lack a common safety protocol.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden to deliver prime-time farewell to nation on Wednesday from Oval Office
item.description: President Joe Biden will deliver a farewell address to the nation Wednesday from the Oval Office, five days before President-elect Donald Trump is sworn in.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Judge holds Giuliani in contempt for defaming Georgia election workers
item.description: A federal judge on Friday held Rudy Giuliani in civil contempt for skirting an agreement not to make new false claims about two Georgia election workers he was ordered to pay $146 million for defaming.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Biden extends time in US for 800,000 Venezuelans, Salvadorans as Trump readies immigration crackdown
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — About 600,000 Venezuelans and more than 230,000 Salvadorans already living in the United States can legally remain another 18 months, the Department of Homeland Security said Friday, barely a week before President-elect Donald Trump takes office with promises of hardline immigration pol
item.dc:creator: GISELA SALOMON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dems eerily silent on Trump sentencing as they prepare for Republican trifecta in Washington
item.description: Democratic members of congress were less reactive to the sentencing of President-elect Trump on Friday, where he was sentenced with no penalty in the New York criminal trial.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: TikTok gets frosty reception at Supreme Court in fight to stave off ban
item.description: TikTok received a frosty reception in its fight to save the platform at the Supreme Court, which during oral arguments Friday expressed sympathy with the government’s national security concerns about the platform’s ties to China.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: President-elect Trump sentenced to unconditional discharge in hush money case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump was sentenced Friday in his hush money case, but the judge declined to impose any punishment.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Investigation clears Duluth mayor’s campaign manager and partner
item.description: An external investigation commissioned by the city of Duluth has found Mayor Roger Reinert’s partner and campaign manager Amber Gurske did not act improperly through her communications with city staff early in his administration.
item.dc:creator: Dan Kraker, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court appears skeptical of blocking US ban on TiKTok: What to know
item.description: The Supreme Court heard TikTok's case to toss out a ban just nine days before it will take effect. The Biden administration defended the measure on national security grounds.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate GOP fears land mines in House could derail Trump's tax agenda
item.description: Senate Republicans fear President-elect Trump’s tax agenda could be derailed in the House by several potential land mines, including calls by some GOP lawmakers to raise corporate taxes and to lift the cap on state and local tax (SALT) deductions.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jimmy Carter's funeral brings together 5 current and former US presidents to honor one of their own
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As they filed into the front pews at Washington National Cathedral, wearing dark suits and mostly solemn faces, five current and former presidents came together for Jimmy Carter's funeral.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Memo: Trump-Newsom feud ramps up as Los Angeles fires rage on
item.description: The feud between President-elect Trump and California Gov. Gavin Newsom (D) is hitting new peaks of intensity as wildfires rage on in Los Angeles. Trump has called on Newsom to resign, while the governor has hit back at Trump for politicizing the disaster.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: LA Mayor Bass stonewalls reporter for 90 seconds at airport
item.description: Video taken on an airport jetway shows Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass repeatedly ignoring questions from a reporter about her response to the wildfires ripping through the area this week.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Pennsylvania GOP Rep. Meuser considering run for governor
item.description: Rep. Dan Meuser (R-Pa.) signaled he is seriously considering challenging Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro (D) for his post in 2026. “I’m considering it,” Meuser told The Hill on Thursday. “I mean if I get in it’s because I think I can win and that means beating the incumbent which is Josh Shapiro.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Laken Riley Act overwhelmingly clears first hurdle in Senate with help from Democrats
item.description: The Senate on Thursday overwhelmingly cleared a key procedural hurdle toward passing the Laken Riley Act, a bill aimed at curbing crime by migrants, in the first vote of the year by the chamber.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Michelle Obama 'sends her thoughts and prayers' while absent from Carter's funeral
item.description: Michelle Obama wasn't among the mourners attending former President Carter's funeral service in Washington, but she says she's thinking of and praying for "everyone who loved and learned" from the late commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: Lack of federal funds may mean no EBT replacements for scam victims
item.description: Many parents have reported benefits being erased from EBT cards in the weeks since Congress declined to continue funding.
item.dc:creator: Sean C. Davis, NewsNation

item.title: Gingrich predicts Johnson will be Speaker for 6 years
item.description: Former Speaker Newt Gingrich (R-Ga.) is predicting that Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) will remain in the top job for six years.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: What's next for Canada? 5 questions will help decide the future after Trudeau
item.description: "We're in a unique situation in Canadian politics," Shachi Kurl, president of the Angus Reid Institute, tells NPR. Voters need to replace a lame-duck leader — and cope with threats from Trump.
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell, MPR News

item.title: Ex-FBI informant who fabricated bribery story about Biden and his son Hunter gets 6 years in prison
item.description: A former FBI informant who fabricated a story about President Joe Biden and his son Hunter accepting bribes that became central to Republicans’ impeachment effort was sentenced Wednesday to six years in prison.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New book alleges that someone at Fox News gave Trump's team early look at town hall questions
item.description: Someone inside Fox News gave Donald Trump's presidential campaign the questions he would face ahead of a town hall the network produced in January 2024, a forthcoming book alleges, according to excerpts reported by CNN.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan J. Cooper, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden says he could've beaten Trump in November
item.description: “It’s presumptuous to say that, but I think yes,” the president said when reporter Susan Page asked if he could have won.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: These Democrats flipped their votes on the Laken Riley Act
item.description: Seven House Democrats flipped their votes to "yes" on a GOP-led bill named after the slain Georgia student Laken Riley that would require detention of migrants arrested for theft.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Freedom Caucus holds its fire on Johnson’s strategy for passing Trump agenda
item.description: Members of the conservative House Freedom Caucus are holding their fire despite Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) bucking their preferred strategy for passing President-elect Trump’s agenda, arguing that the ultimate product is more important than the configuration.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks release of special counsel report on Trump cases as court fight simmers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Tuesday temporarily blocked the public release of special counsel Jack Smith's report on investigations into Donald Trump as an appeals court weighs a challenge to the disclosure of a much-anticipated document just days before the president-elect reclaims office.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Biden creates 2 new national monuments, setting a conservation record
item.description: The White House says President Joe Biden has now protected a total of 674 million acres of lands and waters — a record for any administration. This includes two new national monuments in California.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: Rudy Giuliani is held in contempt of court in $148 million defamation case
item.description: Rudy Giuliani was found in contempt of court for failing to properly respond to requests for information as he turned over assets to satisfy a defamation ruling granted to two Georgia poll workers.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Hush money judge refuses to delay Trump's criminal sentencing
item.description: The New York judge who oversaw President-elect Trump’s criminal trial last spring declined his request to pause Friday’s sentencing, setting up a last-minute appellate battle as he seeks to stave off the proceeding.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Canada's Trudeau announces resignation after nearly a decade as prime minister
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau announced his resignation Monday after nearly a decade in power, bowing to rising discontent over his leadership and growing turmoil within his government signaled by the abrupt departure of his finance minister.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Pence: 'Admirable' that Harris certified Trump's win after losing election
item.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence, who had to be evacuated four years ago Monday as rioters stormed the Capitol, welcomed "the return of order and civility" as Congress moved to certify President-elect Donald Trump's 2024 victory.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Canadian PM Justin Trudeau resigns
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau announced his resignation Monday in the face of rising discontent over his leadership, and after the abrupt departure of his finance minister signaled growing turmoil within his government.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Wisconsin’s Evers revives plan to enable voters to repeal and create state laws
item.description: Wisconsin Gov. Tony Evers is reviving his plan to enable voters to repeal and create state laws, this time through the state budget.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Jimmy Carter's state funeral starts Saturday. Here is what to know
item.description: Six days of funeral observances for former President Jimmy Carter begin Saturday in Georgia, where he died on Dec. 29 at the age of 100.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, WFLA, NBC
